 {"id":"bd-29lr","title":"Phase 1: Foundation - fastapi-output Crate and Core Infrastructure","description":"# Phase 1: Foundation

## Purpose

Establish the core infrastructure for rich output integration by creating the new fastapi-output 
crate with agent detection, mode switching, and the RichOutput facade API.

## Why This Phase First

Everything else depends on this foundation:
- Agent detection determines which mode to use
- OutputMode enum is used by all components
- RichOutput facade is the unified API all other phases call
- Theme system defines colors used everywhere

Without this phase, no other rich output work can proceed.

## Deliverables

1. **New crate: fastapi-output** (crates/fastapi-output/)
   - Cargo.toml with rich_rust as optional dependency
   - Module structure for all subcomponents
   - Re-exports for convenient API access

2. **Agent Detection System** (detection.rs)
   - is_agent_environment() function
   - Environment variable checks (CLAUDE_CODE, CODEX_CLI, etc.)
   - TTY detection via crossterm
   - NO_COLOR standard support
   - FORCE_COLOR override

3. **OutputMode System** (mode.rs)
   - OutputMode enum (Rich, Plain, Minimal)
   - Mode selection logic
   - Runtime mode switching support

4. **RichOutput Facade** (facade.rs)
   - Central RichOutput struct
   - Mode-aware method dispatching
   - Global instance management (lazy static)
   - Builder pattern for configuration

5. **Theme System** (themes.rs)
   - FastApiTheme struct with all color definitions
   - ThemePreset enum for built-in themes
   - Color constants for HTTP methods, status codes
   - Theme loading and application

## Technical Decisions

### Why a Separate Crate?

1. **Modularity**: Can be disabled entirely via feature flag
2. **Compile Time**: Only compiled when needed
3. **Dependency Isolation**: rich_rust dependency contained
4. **Testing**: Can test output logic independently

### Why Optional rich_rust Dependency?

When the "rich" feature is disabled:
- No rich_rust code compiled
- Plain mode is the only mode
- Minimal binary size impact
- Zero runtime overhead

### Global Instance vs Passed Reference?

Using a global lazy_static instance because:
- Output happens everywhere in the codebase
- Passing RichOutput through every function is impractical
- One-time initialization is fine for this use case
- Thread-safe via LazyLock

## Files to Create

```
crates/fastapi-output/
├── Cargo.toml
├── src/
│   ├── lib.rs           # Public API, prelude
│   ├── detection.rs     # Agent environment detection
│   ├── mode.rs          # OutputMode enum and logic
│   ├── facade.rs        # RichOutput struct
│   └── themes.rs        # Theme definitions
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. `cargo build -p fastapi-output` succeeds
2. `cargo build -p fastapi-output --no-default-features` succeeds (plain mode only)
3. Agent detection correctly identifies Claude Code, Codex, etc.
4. Mode switching works at runtime
5. Theme colors render correctly in rich mode
6. Unit tests pass for all detection heuristics","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:02:53.810604677Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:44:13.642586798Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:44:13.642507057Z","close_reason":"Foundation checks done; output crate builds with and without rich; FORCE_COLOR override added","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation","phase-1","rich-output"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-29lr","depends_on_id":"bd-nww8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:25:13Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2buz","title":"Implement OpenAPI endpoint table display","description":"## Task: OpenAPI Endpoint Table Display

### Parent Feature
Phase 5: OpenAPI Display & Visual Polish (bd-urty)

### Overview
Create a visual table display of all OpenAPI-documented endpoints, showing methods, paths, summaries, and tags in a scannable format.

### Background & Reasoning
When developing APIs, having a quick overview of all endpoints helps:
- Verify all routes are documented
- Check for naming consistency
- Understand API scope at a glance
- Debug missing endpoints

### Technical Implementation

#### Data Structures
```rust
// crates/fastapi-output/src/components/openapi_display.rs

pub struct OpenApiEndpointDisplay {
    pub endpoints: Vec<EndpointInfo>,
    pub group_by_tag: bool,
    pub show_responses: bool,
}

pub struct EndpointInfo {
    pub method: HttpMethod,
    pub path: String,
    pub operation_id: Option<String>,
    pub summary: Option<String>,
    pub tags: Vec<String>,
    pub deprecated: bool,
    pub auth_required: bool,
    pub response_codes: Vec<u16>,
}
```

#### Visualization

**Rich Mode**:
```
┌──────────────────────── API Endpoints ────────────────────────┐
│                                                                │
│ 12 endpoints documented                                        │
│                                                                │
│ Users                                                          │
│ ┌────────┬─────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┐│
│ │ GET    │ /users              │ List all users               ││
│ │ POST   │ /users              │ Create a new user     🔒     ││
│ │ GET    │ /users/{id}         │ Get user by ID               ││
│ │ PUT    │ /users/{id}         │ Update user           🔒     ││
│ │ DELETE │ /users/{id}         │ Delete user           🔒     ││
│ └────────┴─────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────┘│
│                                                                │
│ Items                                                          │
│ ┌────────┬─────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┐│
│ │ GET    │ /items              │ List all items               ││
│ │ POST   │ /items              │ Create an item        🔒     ││
│ │ GET    │ /items/{id}         │ Get item by ID               ││
│ └────────┴─────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────┘│
│                                                                │
│ 🔒 = Authentication required                                   │
│ ⚠ = Deprecated                                                │
│                                                                │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Plain Mode**:
```
API Endpoints (12 total):

[Users]
  GET    /users             List all users
  POST   /users             Create a new user [auth]
  GET    /users/{id}        Get user by ID
  PUT    /users/{id}        Update user [auth]
  DELETE /users/{id}        Delete user [auth]

[Items]
  GET    /items             List all items
  POST   /items             Create an item [auth]
  GET    /items/{id}        Get item by ID
```

### Implementation
```rust
impl OpenApiEndpointDisplay {
    pub fn display(&self) {
        let output = RichOutput::global();
        
        if !output.should_render() {
            self.display_plain(&output);
            return;
        }
        
        let theme = output.theme();
        let mut content = Vec::new();
        
        content.push(String::new());
        content.push(format!("  {} endpoints documented", self.endpoints.len()));
        content.push(String::new());
        
        // Group by tag if enabled
        let groups = if self.group_by_tag {
            self.group_endpoints_by_tag()
        } else {
            vec![("All".to_string(), self.endpoints.clone())]
        };
        
        for (tag, endpoints) in groups {
            if self.group_by_tag {
                content.push(theme.style_text(&format!("  {}", tag), "bold"));
            }
            
            let mut table = Table::new()
                .with_column(Column::new("").width(8))
                .with_column(Column::new("").width(25))
                .with_column(Column::new("").max_width(40));
            
            for ep in &endpoints {
                let method_styled = theme.style_method(&ep.method);
                
                let mut summary = ep.summary.clone().unwrap_or_default();
                
                // Add indicators
                let mut indicators = Vec::new();
                if ep.auth_required {
                    indicators.push("🔒");
                }
                if ep.deprecated {
                    indicators.push("⚠");
                }
                
                if !indicators.is_empty() {
                    summary = format!("{} {}", summary, indicators.join(" "));
                }
                
                table.add_row_cells([
                    method_styled,
                    ep.path.clone(),
                    summary,
                ]);
            }
            
            content.push(format!("{}", table.render_to_string()));
            content.push(String::new());
        }
        
        // Legend
        content.push(theme.style_text("  🔒 = Authentication required", "dim"));
        content.push(theme.style_text("  ⚠ = Deprecated", "dim"));
        content.push(String::new());
        
        let panel = Panel::from_text(&content.join("\n"))
            .title("API Endpoints")
            .rounded();
        
        output.renderable(&panel);
    }
}
```

### Integration with OpenAPI Crate
```rust
// In fastapi-openapi/src/lib.rs

impl OpenApiSpec {
    pub fn display_endpoints(&self) {
        let endpoints: Vec<EndpointInfo> = self.paths.iter()
            .flat_map(|(path, ops)| {
                ops.iter().map(|(method, op)| EndpointInfo {
                    method: method.clone(),
                    path: path.clone(),
                    operation_id: op.operation_id.clone(),
                    summary: op.summary.clone(),
                    tags: op.tags.clone(),
                    deprecated: op.deprecated,
                    auth_required: !op.security.is_empty(),
                    response_codes: op.responses.keys().cloned().collect(),
                })
            })
            .collect();
        
        OpenApiEndpointDisplay {
            endpoints,
            group_by_tag: true,
            show_responses: false,
        }.display();
    }
}
```

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] EndpointInfo data structure
- [ ] Table display with method coloring
- [ ] Tag-based grouping (optional)
- [ ] Auth requirement indicator (🔒)
- [ ] Deprecation indicator (⚠)
- [ ] Endpoint count summary
- [ ] Legend for indicators
- [ ] Plain text fallback
- [ ] Integration with OpenAPI spec
- [ ] Unit tests

### Dependencies
- bd-36uw (RichOutput facade)
- bd-14v4 (FastApiTheme)
- bd-urty (Phase 5 feature)

-{"id":"bd-2c9t","title":"HTTP/2: server + framing + HPACK + tests (asupersync-only)","description":"Implement HTTP/2 support without Tokio/Hyper: connection preface, streams, framing, flow control, HPACK header compression, and parity-focused tests.","notes":"2026-02-10 (ChartreuseAnchor):
- Starting bd-2c9t per bv --robot-triage top pick.
- Plan: implement HTTP/2 cleartext (h2c prior knowledge first) in fastapi-http: frame codec + HPACK decoder (incl Huffman) + basic connection state (SETTINGS, stream lifecycle, flow control), then map streams to existing Request/Response and add end-to-end tests using asupersync only.
- First milestone: parse preface + SETTINGS exchange + decode HEADERS for a simple GET with non-huffman literals; respond with minimal headers + DATA.


2026-02-10 (WildCave):
- Implemented HTTP/2 h2c prior-knowledge sniffing + server integration (App + Handler + generic process_connection).
- Added HTTP/2 response HPACK encoder (literal without indexing, no huffman) and HEADERS/DATA framing (incl CONTINUATION split, streamed bodies).
- Added E2E TCP test: HTTP/2 preface + SETTINGS + HEADERS GET / (RFC 7541 C.2.1) -> :status 200 + body.
- Quality gates: cargo fmt/check/clippy/test all clean.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-10T06:34:52.947463023Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T08:49:08.803915303Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2c9t","depends_on_id":"bd-uz2s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T06:34:52.953878405Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":61,"issue_id":"bd-2c9t","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"2026-02-11 (CloudyBeaver): bv top picks were bd-3ess/bd-z09e but those edit surfaces are currently held by other agents via exclusive reservations. In crates/fastapi-http/src/server.rs, aligned non-app paths ( and ) with app-path behavior so interleaved control frames during body read are handled instead of hard-failing: inline handling for SETTINGS (apply + ACK), PING (validate + ACK), and GOAWAY (graceful return). Added integration coverage in crates/fastapi-http/tests/http2.rs: , which exercises  path with HEADERS(no END_STREAM) + interleaved PING + DATA END_STREAM and asserts PING ACK plus successful 200 response/body. Validation: cargo test -p fastapi-http --test http2; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo fmt --check; ubs --diff --only=rust .","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:24:13Z"},{"id":63,"issue_id":"bd-2c9t","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"2026-02-11 (CloudyBeaver) correction: completed HTTP/2 control-frame/body-read parity slice. In crates/fastapi-http/src/server.rs, aligned non-app paths (process_connection_http2 and handle_connection_handler_http2) with app-path behavior so interleaved control frames during body read are handled instead of hard-failing: inline handling for SETTINGS (apply + ACK), PING (validate + ACK), and GOAWAY (graceful return). Added integration coverage in crates/fastapi-http/tests/http2.rs: http2_handler_path_allows_interleaved_ping_while_reading_body, which exercises serve_on_handler path with HEADERS(no END_STREAM) + interleaved PING + DATA END_STREAM and asserts PING ACK plus successful 200 response/body. Validation: cargo test -p fastapi-http --test http2; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo fmt --check; ubs --diff --only=rust .","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:24:28Z"},{"id":65,"issue_id":"bd-2c9t","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"2026-02-11 (CloudyBeaver) follow-up: added coverage for the second patched HTTP/2 path. New integration test in crates/fastapi-http/tests/http2.rs named http2_closure_path_allows_interleaved_ping_while_reading_body exercises serve_on closure path (process_connection_http2) with HEADERS(no END_STREAM) + interleaved PING + DATA END_STREAM, asserting PING ACK and successful 200 response/body. This complements existing handler-path test so both non-app loops now have E2E coverage. Validation rerun: cargo test -p fastapi-http --test http2; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo fmt --check; ubs --diff --only=rust .","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:26:22Z"},{"id":71,"issue_id":"bd-2c9t","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"2026-02-11 (ChartreuseAnchor): starting interleaved WINDOW_UPDATE parity slice (server.rs + tests/http2.rs). Current bug: request-body loops reject WINDOW_UPDATE as unsupported; patching to accept/validate interleaved WINDOW_UPDATE and adding regressions for app/handler/closure paths.","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:30:49Z"},{"id":72,"issue_id":"bd-2c9t","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"2026-02-11 (ChartreuseAnchor): fixed interleaved WINDOW_UPDATE handling while reading HTTP/2 request bodies across all three server paths (app/handler/closure) in crates/fastapi-http/src/server.rs. Added payload validation helper (len=4, increment!=0) and integration regressions in crates/fastapi-http/tests/http2.rs: http2_app_path_allows_interleaved_window_update_while_reading_body, http2_handler_path_allows_interleaved_window_update_while_reading_body, http2_closure_path_allows_interleaved_window_update_while_reading_body. Added unit tests for validate_window_update_payload. Validation: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test -p fastapi-http window_update_payload_validation; cargo test -p fastapi-http --test http2; ubs --diff --only=rust . (critical=0).","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:33:22Z"},{"id":74,"issue_id":"bd-2c9t","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"2026-02-11 (CloudyBeaver): starting next bd-2c9t hardening slice. Gap targeted: interleaved PRIORITY frames during HTTP/2 request-body read are currently treated as unsupported/protocol error in body-read loops. Planned change: accept+validate PRIORITY interleaves in all 3 loops, add helper/unit tests in server.rs and E2E regressions in crates/fastapi-http/tests/http2.rs.","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:30:27Z"},{"id":75,"issue_id":"bd-2c9t","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"2026-02-11 (CloudyBeaver): completed interleaved PRIORITY parity slice. In crates/fastapi-http/src/server.rs, all three HTTP/2 request-body read loops now accept PRIORITY alongside SETTINGS/PING/GOAWAY/WINDOW_UPDATE and validate PRIORITY payload semantics. Added validate_priority_payload(stream_id,payload) with checks: stream id must be non-zero, payload length must be 5 bytes, and dependency stream must not equal the prioritized stream. Added unit tests in server.rs for valid and invalid PRIORITY payloads. Added E2E regressions in crates/fastapi-http/tests/http2.rs for app/handler/closure paths: http2_app_path_allows_interleaved_priority_while_reading_body, http2_handler_path_allows_interleaved_priority_while_reading_body, http2_closure_path_allows_interleaved_priority_while_reading_body. Validation: cargo test -p fastapi-http --test http2; cargo test -p fastapi-http priority_payload_validation -- --nocapture; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo fmt --check; ubs --diff --only=rust .","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:32:13Z"},{"id":76,"issue_id":"bd-2c9t","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"2026-02-12 (ChartreuseAnchor): starting RST_STREAM interleaving parity slice. Goal: request-body read loops should treat same-stream RST_STREAM as stream-local abort (not connection-level protocol error), with regressions demonstrating connection stays alive for subsequent streams.","created_at":"2026-02-12T05:43:47Z"},{"id":77,"issue_id":"bd-2c9t","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"2026-02-12 (ChartreuseAnchor): implemented stream-local RST_STREAM handling during HTTP/2 request-body reads across app/handler/closure server paths. Same-stream RST_STREAM now aborts only that stream (skip handler/response), while connection remains available for subsequent streams. Added validate_rst_stream_payload(stream_id,payload) and unit tests. Added E2E regressions in tests/http2.rs proving stream 1 reset does not prevent successful stream 3 request on same connection.","created_at":"2026-02-12T05:46:04Z"},{"id":79,"issue_id":"bd-2c9t","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"2026-02-12 (ChartreuseAnchor): starting SETTINGS ACK payload-validation slice. Enforcing RFC behavior that ACKed SETTINGS frames must carry empty payload across HTTP/2 handling paths; adding regressions.","created_at":"2026-02-12T05:47:37Z"},{"id":80,"issue_id":"bd-2c9t","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"2026-02-12 (ChartreuseAnchor): landed SETTINGS ACK validation hardening + additional HTTP/2 regressions. Added validate_settings_frame helper and enforced ACK+payload rejection in all HTTP/2 settings handling branches (app/handler/closure + body-read loops). Added runtime tests rejecting non-empty SETTINGS ACK during body-read for app/handler/closure. Also includes recent RST_STREAM stream-local abort handling continuity tests. Commits now on main: 720b16e, 276b73e.","created_at":"2026-02-12T05:50:51Z"},{"id":82,"issue_id":"bd-2c9t","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"2026-02-13 (SapphireHeron): HTTP/2 unknown-extension interleave parity slice. While reading request bodies, app/handler/closure paths now treat FrameType::Unknown as ignorable (discarded) instead of raising protocol error. Added E2E regressions in crates/fastapi-http/tests/http2.rs: http2_app_path_allows_interleaved_unknown_extension_while_reading_body, http2_handler_path_allows_interleaved_unknown_extension_while_reading_body, http2_closure_path_allows_interleaved_unknown_extension_while_reading_body. Validation: cargo test -p fastapi-http --test http2; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo fmt --check.","created_at":"2026-02-13T08:49:08Z"}]}

New integration test in crates/fastapi-http/tests/http2.rs named http2_closure_path_allows_interleaved_ping_while_reading_body exercises serve_on closure path (process_connection_http2) with HEADERS(no END_STREAM) + interleaved PING + DATA END_STREAM, asserting PING ACK and successful 200 response/body. This complements existing handler-path test so both non-app loops now have E2E coverage. Validation rerun: cargo test -p fastapi-http --test http2; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo fmt --check; ubs --diff --only=rust .","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:26:22Z"},{"id":71,"issue_id":"bd-2c9t","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"2026-02-11 (ChartreuseAnchor): starting interleaved WINDOW_UPDATE parity slice (server.rs + tests/http2.rs). Current bug: request-body loops reject WINDOW_UPDATE as unsupported; patching to accept/validate interleaved WINDOW_UPDATE and adding regressions for app/handler/closure paths.","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:30:49Z"},{"id":72,"issue_id":"bd-2c9t","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"2026-02-11 (ChartreuseAnchor): fixed interleaved WINDOW_UPDATE handling while reading HTTP/2 request bodies across all three server paths (app/handler/closure) in crates/fastapi-http/src/server.rs. Added payload validation helper (len=4, increment!=0) and integration regressions in crates/fastapi-http/tests/http2.rs: http2_app_path_allows_interleaved_window_update_while_reading_body, http2_handler_path_allows_interleaved_window_update_while_reading_body, http2_closure_path_allows_interleaved_window_update_while_reading_body. Added unit tests for validate_window_update_payload. Validation: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test -p fastapi-http window_update_payload_validation; cargo test -p fastapi-http --test http2; ubs --diff --only=rust . (critical=0).","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:33:22Z"},{"id":74,"issue_id":"bd-2c9t","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"2026-02-11 (CloudyBeaver): starting next bd-2c9t hardening slice. Gap targeted: interleaved PRIORITY frames during HTTP/2 request-body read are currently treated as unsupported/protocol error in body-read loops. Planned change: accept+validate PRIORITY interleaves in all 3 loops, add helper/unit tests in server.rs and E2E regressions in crates/fastapi-http/tests/http2.rs.","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:30:27Z"},{"id":75,"issue_id":"bd-2c9t","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"2026-02-11 (CloudyBeaver): completed interleaved PRIORITY parity slice. In crates/fastapi-http/src/server.rs, all three HTTP/2 request-body read loops now accept PRIORITY alongside SETTINGS/PING/GOAWAY/WINDOW_UPDATE and validate PRIORITY payload semantics. Added validate_priority_payload(stream_id,payload) with checks: stream id must be non-zero, payload length must be 5 bytes, and dependency stream must not equal the prioritized stream. Added unit tests in server.rs for valid and invalid PRIORITY payloads. Added E2E regressions in crates/fastapi-http/tests/http2.rs for app/handler/closure paths: http2_app_path_allows_interleaved_priority_while_reading_body, http2_handler_path_allows_interleaved_priority_while_reading_body, http2_closure_path_allows_interleaved_priority_while_reading_body. Validation: cargo test -p fastapi-http --test http2; cargo test -p fastapi-http priority_payload_validation -- --nocapture; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo fmt --check; ubs --diff --only=rust .","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:32:13Z"},{"id":76,"issue_id":"bd-2c9t","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"2026-02-12 (ChartreuseAnchor): starting RST_STREAM interleaving parity slice. Goal: request-body read loops should treat same-stream RST_STREAM as stream-local abort (not connection-level protocol error), with regressions demonstrating connection stays alive for subsequent streams.","created_at":"2026-02-12T05:43:47Z"},{"id":77,"issue_id":"bd-2c9t","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"2026-02-12 (ChartreuseAnchor): implemented stream-local RST_STREAM handling during HTTP/2 request-body reads across app/handler/closure server paths. Same-stream RST_STREAM now aborts only that stream (skip handler/response), while connection remains available for subsequent streams. Added validate_rst_stream_payload(stream_id,payload) and unit tests. Added E2E regressions in tests/http2.rs proving stream 1 reset does not prevent successful stream 3 request on same connection.","created_at":"2026-02-12T05:46:04Z"},{"id":79,"issue_id":"bd-2c9t","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"2026-02-12 (ChartreuseAnchor): starting SETTINGS ACK payload-validation slice. Enforcing RFC behavior that ACKed SETTINGS frames must carry empty payload across HTTP/2 handling paths; adding regressions.","created_at":"2026-02-12T05:47:37Z"},{"id":80,"issue_id":"bd-2c9t","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"2026-02-12 (ChartreuseAnchor): landed SETTINGS ACK validation hardening + additional HTTP/2 regressions. Added validate_settings_frame helper and enforced ACK+payload rejection in all HTTP/2 settings handling branches (app/handler/closure + body-read loops). Added runtime tests rejecting non-empty SETTINGS ACK during body-read for app/handler/closure. Also includes recent RST_STREAM stream-local abort handling continuity tests. Commits now on main: 720b16e, 276b73e.","created_at":"2026-02-12T05:50:51Z"},{"id":82,"issue_id":"bd-2c9t","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"2026-02-13 (SapphireHeron): HTTP/2 unknown-extension interleave parity slice. While reading request bodies, app/handler/closure paths now treat FrameType::Unknown as ignorable (discarded) instead of raising protocol error. Added E2E regressions in crates/fastapi-http/tests/http2.rs: http2_app_path_allows_interleaved_unknown_extension_while_reading_body, http2_handler_path_allows_interleaved_unknown_extension_while_reading_body, http2_closure_path_allows_interleaved_unknown_extension_while_reading_body. Validation: cargo test -p fastapi-http --test http2; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo fmt --check.","created_at":"2026-02-13T08:49:08Z"},{"id":83,"issue_id":"bd-2c9t","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Flow control implementation complete: H2FlowControl struct with connection/stream window tracking, WINDOW_UPDATE frame emission wired into all 3 HTTP/2 server paths. 10 new tests (7 unit + 3 E2E) all passing. Quality gates clean.","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:10:31Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2egp","title":"Test SecurityHeaders middleware","description":"## Task
Test all security headers are properly set.

## Implementation
- Test SecurityHeadersConfig builder
- Test each header individually
- Test header combinations
- Test header override behavior

## Headers to Test
1. X-Frame-Options (DENY, SAMEORIGIN)
2. X-Content-Type-Options (nosniff)
3. X-XSS-Protection
4. Referrer-Policy (all modes)
5. Content-Security-Policy
6. Strict-Transport-Security
7. Permissions-Policy
8. Cache-Control for sensitive endpoints

## Location
